notes_for_consumers: Feeds environment prompts and footage queries. Pick settings, objects, and signifiers from the resolved block; lane content is additive on the baseline. Confidence flags drive draft-system review priority. Settings-and-objects only; sensory mood lives in creative_intent. The 'no people in frame' rule is honored absolutely — multigenerational/gathering evidence is rendered through objects (chairs, coolers, table), never bodies. The audience here is Proud Workhorses, the current core.

content.lane_note: Upper end of income for the BC (8th–9th decile, established homeowner with land, often a workshop or pole barn). Maker culture and outdoor recreation deeply present; watercraft and snow-season equipment add a layer absent in lower-latitude BCs.

settings.cue: Lake launch parking lot in season (May–Oct), boat in storage off-season
Hunting camp, deer lease in upper Michigan and Wisconsin
Two-lane through bare deciduous and dark evergreen in winter, lake-effect snowbanks
Feed-store and Tractor Supply parking lot, rural
Workshop with woodworking and metalworking setup
Lakeside cottage or cabin, modest, year-round-use

objects.cue: Boat trailer with fishing boat or pontoon in summer, wrapped or tarped in winter
Plow attachment on the truck or in the workshop
Ice-fishing gear: auger, tip-ups, shanty, sled
Firewood pile, stacked under tarp or in lean-to
John Deere riding mower or compact tractor
Bait shop signage, tackle, leeches and minnow buckets in season
Salt-streaked truck bed and side panels in winter
Hunting orange in fall, decoys for duck and goose

signifiers.cue: Snow on truck, fresh tracks across a snow-covered driveway
Tow scenario engaged: boat in summer, snowmobile or ice-fishing shanty in winter, utility trailer year-round
Cabin window or barn door open into the workshop in cold weather, light from inside
